sections_cited: section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI ITRA 346 of 2017 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on CMA No.4742/2024. 2. For hearing of main case. 18.11.2025 This reference application is pending since 2017 without any progress. On each date time has been sought or the applicant has remained unrepresented without intimation or justification. On 24.02.2021, following order was passed: “24.02.2021 Mr. Kashif Nazeer, advocate for applicant 1. Granted subject to all just exceptions. 2. Learned counsel for the applicant, while confronted as to maintainability of instant reference, which appears to have been filed against an order passed on M.A. (Rectification) and not against a main order from which the proposed question appears to have arisen, has requested for time to assist the court. Time is granted. Adjourned.” Today applicant remains unrepresented without intimation or justification. It appears that the applicant is not interested in the present proceedings. Accordingly, in view of judgment of CIR vs. Rafeh Limited reported as PLD 2020 SC 518, the reference application is dismissed for non-prosecution. A copy of this decision may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance, 2001. Judge Judge Khuhro/PS
